_source: Hooktheory database analysis, Spotify hit analysis, Music theory curricula
_note: Long-tail song structure data. Genre-specific templates with commercial success patterns.

standard_pop.template: Intro → Verse 1 → Pre-Chorus → Chorus → Verse 2 → Pre-Chorus → Chorus → Bridge → Chorus (x2) → Outro

function_of_each.intro: Set mood, introduce sonic palette; 8-16 bars
function_of_each.verse: Tell the story; grow in energy each verse; varies lyrically
function_of_each.pre_chorus: Build tension; raises expectation; shorter bars
function_of_each.chorus: Emotional peak; most memorable; hooky title lyric; repeats
function_of_each.bridge: Contrast and relief; different key or groove; fresh perspective
function_of_each.outro: Wind down; can be fadeout or definitive end

standard_pop.hit_pattern: Chorus first (or within 45s) = higher streaming completion rate (Spotify data)

verse_chorus.use: Country, rock, folk; straightforward; relies on strong hook

aaba_32_bar.template: A (8 bars) → A (8 bars) → B (8 bars bridge) → A (8 bars)
aaba_32_bar.use: Jazz standards, Tin Pan Alley, Great American Songbook
aaba_32_bar.B_section_function: Release tension built in A sections; often goes to relative major/minor

variations.quick_four: IV chord in bar 2; adds early movement
variations.jazz_blues: Replace turnaround with ii-V-I; adds sophistication

four_on_floor_standard.intro: 16-32 bars; beats only or minimal elements
four_on_floor_standard.breakdown_1: 32 bars; melodic elements introduced; no kick
four_on_floor_standard.build: 16-32 bars; riser, snare roll, filtering; energy builds
four_on_floor_standard.break: 16-32 bars; reduce to half or minimal
four_on_floor_standard.drop_2: 32-64 bars; can be variation of drop 1

edm_structures.tension_release: EDM is fundamentally about building and releasing tension; drop is the payoff

hip_hop_structure.typical: Intro → Verse 1 (16 bars) → Hook (8 bars) → Verse 2 (16 bars) → Hook → Bridge/Verse 3 → Hook → Outro

bar_counts.verse: 16 bars standard; 8 bars for shorter modern flows; 32 bars for classics
bar_counts.hook_chorus: 8 bars; 4 bars can work for repetitive trap hooks
bar_counts.bridge: Rare in hip-hop; when used, often instrumental or outro verse

hip_hop_structure.modern_trap_structure: Very hook-forward; hook in first 30s; short verses (8-12 bars); multiple hook repetitions

ambient_and_electronic.no_structure: Ambient often deliberately avoids structure; continuous evolution
ambient_and_electronic.drone_progression: Slowly evolve one element at a time; patience is the technique
ambient_and_electronic.arrival_composition: Build toward one moment of resolution; entire track is the journey (Burial, Arca)

emotional_associations.C_major: Pure, innocent, simple; nursery rhymes, beginner exercises
emotional_associations.A_major: Joyful, open; guitar-friendly; 'Here Comes the Sun'
emotional_associations.A_minor: Dark, tender, melancholic; most common minor for pop
emotional_associations.D_minor: Saddest key (Mozart's opinion); minor with depth

modulation_techniques.common_tone: Move to new key sharing a chord; smooth transition
modulation_techniques.chromatic: Step up half-step; dramatic lift; pop technique (last chorus modulation)
modulation_techniques.pivot_chord: Chord functional in both keys; use it to bridge; most musical
modulation_techniques.direct_modulation: Abrupt cut to new key; jarring but can be effective
